body:
## Summary

Closes #1780. Extends the Telegram `/stop` command so it accepts an issue or pull-request URL in addition to a session UUID. The bot now looks the URL up in the in-memory solve queue and either:

- **cancels the queued item** (action: `cancel-queued`) when the task has not yet started, or
- **forwards CTRL+C via `$ --stop <UUID>`** (action: `stop-running`) when the task is already executing in an isolated session.

URL detection runs **before** the forward/reply rejection used by the chat-level `/stop` pause flow (#1081), so replying with a bare `/stop` to the bot's own `⏳ Waiting (codex queue #2)` queue card just works — the URL is pulled from the replied-to message text.

The UUID flow from #524 is preserved unchanged. Bare `/stop` continues to pause the chat (#1081).

### Resolution priority (UUID > URL, argument > reply)

| Source                  | Result                                            |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| UUID in `/stop` arg     | `$ --stop <UUID>` — most specific                 |
| UUID in replied message | `$ --stop <UUID>` — fallback for queue cards w/ UUID |
| URL in `/stop` arg      | queue lookup by URL                               |
| URL in replied message  | queue lookup by URL — the typical user flow       |
| (none)                  | chat-level pause (existing #1081 behavior)        |

UUIDs win because they are globally unique; a single URL may map to several in-flight requests if a user enqueued the same issue twice.

## What changed

- `src/telegram-start-stop-command.lib.mjs`
  - New exported `extractStopTarget(text, repliedTo)` returning `{ kind: 'uuid'|'url'|null, value, source }`.
  - New private `findFirstIssueOrPullUrl(text)` that walks whitespace-split words and returns the first URL that `parseGitHubUrl()` recognises as `type === 'issue'` or `type === 'pull'`.
  - New private `resolveQueueLookupForUrl(url)` that dispatches on five outcomes: `no-queue`, `not-found`, `cancel-queued`, `stop-running`, `running-not-isolated`.
  - Refactored `bot.command('stop')` body into `authorizeTargetedStop()` and `runStopIsolatedSessionFlow()` helpers (used by both UUID and URL paths).
  - Verbose log lines at every detection / dispatch branch.
  - `extractStopSessionId()` is preserved for backwards compatibility with the existing #524 test suite.
- `src/telegram-bot.mjs` — threads `getSolveQueue` into `registerStartStopCommands(bot, { ...sharedCommandOpts, getSolveQueue })`.
- `tests/test-issue-1780-stop-by-url.mjs` — 29 new unit tests covering the resolver and every dispatcher branch with a stub queue (queued, processing-with-isolation, processing-without-isolation, not-found, no-queue, owner-only, old-message, UUID-in-reply-wins-over-URL-in-arg).
- `package.json` — version bump 1.69.5 → 1.69.6.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-1780/` — issue raw data, both screenshots from the issue, research-sources.json, and a README that walks through requirements, timeline, root causes, and the solution.

## How to reproduce (the bug, before this PR)

1. While Codex is throttled, run `/codex https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 --think max --auto-merge`. The bot posts:
   > ⏳ Waiting (codex queue #2)
   > Issue: https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123
2. Reply `/stop` to that queue card. Nothing happens. The user has no UUID to copy because the task has never been started.

## How to verify (after this PR)

Same setup → reply `/stop` to the queue card. Bot acknowledges:
> 🗑 Removed queued task for `https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123` from `codex` queue.

Direct form also works: `/stop https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123`.

For a task already running with `--isolation screen`: reply `/stop` to any message containing the URL → bot forwards CTRL+C via `$ --stop <UUID>` (the same path the UUID flow uses).

## Test plan

- [x] `node tests/test-issue-524-stop-uuid.mjs` — 25/25 passing (no regression).
- [x] `node tests/test-issue-1780-stop-by-url.mjs` — 29/29 passing (new test suite for the URL flow + resolver).
- [x] `npm test` — full default suite (203 files) passes.
- [x] `npm run lint` — clean.
title: Stop tasks by issue or PR URL
